mnemonics.title: Connectivity Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: CONNECTED: Can One Navigate Path Between Every Distinct pair - If YES, graph is connected!

mnemonics.title: Component Count Formula
mnemonics.content: Components = Vertices - Rank of Incidence Matrix (for connected graph = 1)

mnemonics.title: Tree Properties
mnemonics.content: TREE: Totally Restricted Edges (n-1) - Acyclic, Connected, Edges=n-1

acronyms.usage: Used to denote all vertices in graph G
Used to denote all edges in graph G
Minimum vertices to remove to disconnect G
Minimum edges to remove to disconnect G
Smallest degree among all vertices in G
Largest degree among all vertices in G
Number of edges incident to vertex v

visualTips.visualization: Draw connected graph as one piece of clay - you can travel anywhere. Disconnected graph is like separate pieces you can't cross between.
Imagine flood-filling from any vertex. Whatever gets colored is one component. Repeat until all vertices colored.
A vertex standing alone with no roads connecting to it - degree 0.
A vertex with only one road leading to it - dead end road.

analogies.analogy: Like a city where you can drive from any house to any other house using roads.
Like separate islands - you need a boat (edge between components) to go from one to another.
Each connected piece of a jigsaw puzzle - you can't connect pieces from different components.
Google Maps route - sequence of roads (edges) connecting locations (vertices).
